steps.title: Identify the structural relationship
steps.content: The main clause 'divergent strategies for preserving milk [blank]' is followed by a full independent clause beginning with 'whereas' that elaborates on what those divergent strategies actually were (rennet-coagulation in Europe/Southwest Asia versus acid-heat coagulation on the steppe).

steps.title: Apply the colon rule for elaboration
steps.content:
A colon introduces an independent clause that elaborates on or explains the preceding statement. Here, the 'whereas' clause spells out the two divergent strategies, so a colon is the correct punctuation.
  • A ('emerged' with no punctuation) creates a fused sentence by jamming two independent clauses together.
  • B ('emerged and') is ungrammatical: 'and' cannot connect to a 'whereas' clause, and there is still no punctuation to separate the clauses.
  • D ('emerged,') creates a comma splice, since a single comma is too weak to join two independent clauses.
  • C ('emerged:') correctly introduces the elaborating independent clause.
That confirms C is correct.
